text: The surgeon introduces the endobag into the abdominal cavity, rolling it open and positioning it within the pelvis. The ovary containing the dermoid cyst is carefully placed inside the endobag, emphasizing gentle handling throughout the procedure.
The laparoscopic view reveals the left ovary with a visible dermoid cyst measuring approximately four by three centimeters.

The surgeon uses cold knife scissors to create a dissection plane over the ovarian cortex at its most prominent point, allowing the cyst wall to be opened. The cyst wall is then gently extended using a laparoscopic shearer.

The surgeon performs enucleation of the dermoid cyst by applying gentle traction and counter-traction, ensuring the cyst remains intact without rupturing. The cystectomy is conducted within the endobag to prevent spillage of its contents.

The surgeon completes the bloodless dissection of the dermoid cyst within the endobag. The cyst contents are carefully spilled open inside the bag, preventing spillage into the peritoneum. Suction aspiration is used to aspirate the contents from the bag.
After aspirating the cyst contents, the surgeon completes the cystectomy. The ovarian bed is then visually examined for any signs of bleeding or spurting vessels.

The surgeon retrieves the small cyst from inside the endobag through a ten mm suprapubic port, removing it from the peritoneal cavity. A gentle peritoneal lavage is performed, followed by re-aspiration to ensure any spilled contents are removed. The ovarian bed is shown with complete hemostasis, without any visible bleeding.
